input: Classify this waste item: Syringes

output.reason: Medical sharps are biohazardous and require special handling to prevent injury and infection.
preparation_steps: Store used sharps in a leak-resistant container, such as an FDA-approved red sharps disposal box or a heavy plastic bottle.
Seal the container when it is three-quarters full and label it 'DO NOT RECYCLE: SHARPS'.
output.safety_notes: Handle medical sharps with care to prevent injury and infection. Never throw away or flush medical sharps.

input: Classify this waste item: Tablets

output.reason: Small electronics contain heavy metals and other hazardous materials that can contaminate soil and water if not disposed of properly. Recycling them helps to conserve natural resources and reduce waste.
preparation_steps: Factory reset your electronics and delete any pictures or songs from your devices.
Remove the battery if possible, especially for electronics using a lithium-ion battery.
Look for a mail-in program that will pay for small electronics or find charities that will accept them to raise money.
Ask your company's IT department to schedule an electronics recycling event once a year if you work in an office.
output.safety_notes: Special transportation procedure required for lithium-ion batteries.

input: Classify this waste item: those old CFL bulbs

output.reason: CFLs contain mercury, a toxic substance that can harm the environment if not disposed of properly. Recycling CFLs helps to recover mercury and other materials.
preparation_steps: Unplug the lamp or turn off the fuse box before removing the CFL
Put the CFL in a plastic zipper bag to prevent mercury from leaking if it breaks
Do not pack multiple CFLs in the same bag
output.safety_notes: Be careful when handling CFLs as they contain mercury, which can be toxic if ingested or inhaled.

input: Classify this waste item: LCD monitors

output.reason: Computer monitors contain toxic metals like lead and mercury that require special handling and recycling to prevent environmental contamination.

Use a dolly or a rolling chair to transport the monitor to your car.
Place a towel or blanket down in the car and put the monitor face down on top.
output.safety_notes: Handle with care to avoid breaking and potential exposure to toxic materials.

input: Classify this waste item: glass jar thingy

output.reason: Glass bottles and jars can be recycled infinitely with no loss in purity, and represent the quickest recycled-packaging process.

input: Classify this waste item: Rechargeable Batteries

input: Classify this waste item: Unwanted or Expired Medications

output.reason: Medications are hazardous and require special disposal to prevent harm to humans and the environment.
preparation_steps: Pour medication into a sealable plastic bag. If the medication is a solid (pill, liquid capsule, etc.), add water to dissolve it.
Add kitty litter, sawdust or coffee grounds (or any material that mixes with the medication and makes it less appealing for pets and children to eat) to the plastic bag.
Seal the plastic bag and put it in the trash.
Remove and destroy all identifying personal information on the prescription label from all medication containers before recycling them or throwing them away.
output.safety_notes: Responsible medication disposal is crucial for human and environmental health. Follow any disposal instructions on the medication package, and use take-back or mail-back programs if available.

input: Classify this waste item: leftover meds from doctor

output.reason: Medications are hazardous and require special disposal to prevent harm to humans and the environment.
preparation_steps: Pour medication into a sealable plastic bag. If the medication is a solid (pill, liquid capsule, etc.), add water to dissolve it.

Seal the plastic bag and put it in the trash.
Remove and destroy all identifying personal information on the prescription label from all medication containers before recycling them or throwing them away.
output.safety_notes: Responsible medication disposal is crucial for human and environmental health. Follow any disposal instructions on the medication package, and use take-back or mail-back programs if available.

input: Classify this waste item: Transmission Fluid

output.reason: Many automotive fluids like transmission fluid, power steering fluid, gear oil, motor oil, and antifreeze can be processed and recycled into new fluids, preserving local water and soil health by keeping them out of landfills and drains.
preparation_steps: Drain fluids from your vehicle
Place each fluid in a separate container for transportation, do not mix them
Use original motor oil bottles, household containers with secure lids, or sealed specialty containers available at most auto parts stores
output.safety_notes: Improper disposal of used motor oil and other auto fluids can contaminate drinking water and soil, and is considered hazardous waste by many state and local governments.

input: Classify this waste item: Medications

output.reason: Medications are hazardous and require special disposal to prevent harm to humans and the environment.
preparation_steps: Pour medication into a sealable plastic bag. If the medication is a solid (pill, liquid capsule, etc.), add water to dissolve it.
Add kitty litter, sawdust or coffee grounds (or any material that mixes with the medication and makes it less appealing for pets and children to eat) to the plastic bag.
Seal the plastic bag and put it in the trash.
Remove and destroy all identifying personal information on the prescription label from all medication containers before recycling them or throwing them away.
output.safety_notes: Responsible medication disposal is crucial for human and environmental health. Follow any disposal instructions on the medication package, and use take-back or mail-back programs if available.

input: Classify this waste item: Prescription Drugs

output.reason: Medications are hazardous and require special disposal to prevent harm to humans and the environment.
preparation_steps: Pour medication into a sealable plastic bag. If the medication is a solid (pill, liquid capsule, etc.), add water to dissolve it.

Seal the plastic bag and put it in the trash.
Remove and destroy all identifying personal information on the prescription label from all medication containers before recycling them or throwing them away.
output.safety_notes: Responsible medication disposal is crucial for human and environmental health. Follow any disposal instructions on the medication package, and use take-back or mail-back programs if available.

input: Classify this waste item: CFLs

output.reason: CFLs contain 4 milligrams of mercury, which is harmless when the bulb is intact but potentially toxic if the bulb breaks in a landfill and enters the water stream, so recycling them is crucial to prevent mercury pollution and conserve this precious metal.
preparation_steps: Unplug the lamp to prevent electrocution when removing a burnt-out CFL
Put the CFL in a plastic zipper bag to prevent any mercury from leaking if it breaks during transportation
Do not pack multiple CFLs in the same bag, as this will increase the chance of them breaking
output.safety_notes: If the bulb breaks, follow proper clean-up procedures to prevent mercury exposure

input: Classify this waste item: Small Electronics

output.reason: Small electronics contain heavy metals and other hazardous materials that can contaminate soil and water if not disposed of properly. Recycling them helps to conserve natural resources and reduce waste.
preparation_steps: Factory reset your electronics and delete any pictures or songs from your devices.
Remove the battery if possible, especially for electronics using a lithium-ion battery.
Look for a mail-in program that will pay for small electronics or find charities that will accept them to raise money.
Ask your company's IT department to schedule an electronics recycling event once a year if you work in an office.
output.safety_notes: Special transportation procedure required for lithium-ion batteries.

input: Classify this waste item: expired medicines

output.reason: Medications are hazardous and require special disposal to prevent harm to humans and the environment.
preparation_steps: Pour medication into a sealable plastic bag. If the medication is a solid (pill, liquid capsule, etc.), add water to dissolve it.
Add kitty litter, sawdust or coffee grounds (or any material that mixes with the medication and makes it less appealing for pets and children to eat) to the plastic bag.
Seal the plastic bag and put it in the trash.
Remove and destroy all identifying personal information on the prescription label from all medication containers before recycling them or throwing them away.
output.safety_notes: Responsible medication disposal is crucial for human and environmental health. Follow any disposal instructions on the medication package, and use take-back or mail-back programs if available.

Remove any removable parts not made of metal (such as the tempered glass or plastic blender jar).

input: Classify this waste item: my old phone and charger

output.reason: Small electronics contain heavy metals and other hazardous materials that can contaminate soil and water if not disposed of properly. Recycling them helps to conserve natural resources and reduce waste.

output.safety_notes: Special transportation procedure required for lithium-ion batteries.

input: Classify this waste item: those old CFL bulbs

output.reason: CFLs contain 4 milligrams of mercury, which is harmless when the bulb is intact but potentially toxic if the bulb breaks in a landfill and enters the water stream, so recycling them is crucial to prevent mercury pollution and conserve this precious metal.
preparation_steps: Unplug the lamp to prevent electrocution when removing a burnt-out CFL
Put the CFL in a plastic zipper bag to prevent any mercury from leaking if it breaks during transportation
Do not pack multiple CFLs in the same bag, as this will increase the chance of them breaking
output.safety_notes: If the bulb breaks, follow proper clean-up procedures to prevent mercury exposure

input: Classify this waste item: Expired Medications

output.reason: Medications are not recyclable, but responsible disposal is crucial for human and environmental health.

Seal the plastic bag and put it in the trash.
Remove and destroy all identifying personal information on the prescription label from all medication containers before recycling them or throwing them away.

input: Classify this waste item: broken mobile and power bank

output.reason: Small electronics contain heavy metals and other hazardous materials that can contaminate soil and water if not disposed of properly. Recycling them helps to conserve natural resources and reduce waste.
preparation_steps: Factory reset your electronics and delete any pictures or songs from your devices.
Remove the battery if possible, especially for electronics using a lithium-ion battery.
Look for a mail-in program that will pay for small electronics or find charities that will accept them to raise money.
Ask your company's IT department to schedule an electronics recycling event once a year if you work in an office.
output.safety_notes: Special transportation procedure required for lithium-ion batteries.

input: Classify this waste item: Glass Packaging

output.reason: Glass bottles and jars can be recycled infinitely with no loss in purity, and represent the quickest recycled-packaging process.

Remove all wine corks and any non-containers from your glass recycling, such as Pyrex, glassware, windows, and mirrors.

input: Classify this waste item: the oil from last night's cooking

output.reason: Cooking oil can solidify and cause blockages in sewers, but it can be recycled into biodiesel and other products.

input: Classify this waste item: Used motor oil and filters

output.reason: Used motor oil and filters can be recycled to conserve natural resources, reduce energy consumption, and prevent water pollution. Recycling these products also helps to recover valuable materials like steel from oil filters.
preparation_steps: Drain motor oil into a secure container with a top that seals.
Do not mix in any other car fluids like antifreeze or brake fluid.
Use a screwdriver to puncture the dome of your used oil filter, then turn it upside down and let it drain into the motor oil container overnight.
Put your oil filter in a plastic bag once it is fully drained.
output.safety_notes: Do not mix used motor oil with other household hazardous waste, and follow local guidelines for collection and transportation.

input: Classify this waste item: my old engine oil container and filter

output.reason: Used motor oil and filters can be recycled to conserve natural resources, reduce energy consumption, and prevent water pollution. Recycling these products also helps to recover valuable materials like steel from oil filters.

Put your oil filter in a plastic bag once it is fully drained.
output.safety_notes: Do not mix used motor oil with other household hazardous waste, and follow local guidelines for collection and transportation.

Store spent batteries so that their active ends are not touching, either by taping the ends or lining them up in a box.
output.safety_notes: Damaged batteries can be hazardous and may cause fires. Handle them with care and check with your battery recycler for specific instructions.

input: Classify this waste item: Oil Filter Recycling

output.reason: Used motor oil and filters can be recycled to conserve natural resources, reduce energy consumption, and prevent water pollution. Recycling these products also helps to recover valuable materials like steel from oil filters.
preparation_steps: Drain motor oil into a secure container with a top that seals.
Do not mix in any other car fluids like antifreeze or brake fluid.
Use a screwdriver to puncture the dome of your used oil filter, then turn it upside down and let it drain into the motor oil container overnight.
Put your oil filter in a plastic bag once it is fully drained.
output.safety_notes: Do not mix used motor oil with other household hazardous waste, and follow local guidelines for collection and transportation.

input: Classify this waste item: Small Devices

output.safety_notes: Special transportation procedure required for lithium-ion batteries.

input: Classify this waste item: Motor Oil Filters

preparation_steps: Drain motor oil into a secure container with a sealed top.
Do not mix with other car fluids.
Collect oil filters and puncture the dome to drain oil.
Place drained oil filters in a plastic bag.
output.safety_notes: Handle used motor oil and filters with care, as they can cause skin irritation and contain hazardous materials.
